Conference abstracts of this kind are functionally equivalent to a poster/extended abstract and fail the mission's peer-review academic-acceptance rule (arXiv/posters/unreviewed manuscripts are discovery-only).
notes: Scout flagged this venue as ambiguous (journal vs. proceedings) and correctly declined to upgrade; verifier's direct fetch resolved the ambiguity in favor of 'proceedings abstract,' confirming the scout's caution was warranted. Full author list confirmed: Kang To, Han Sun-woo, Kim Kyung-mo, Kim Hun-hee; affiliations Korea Atomic Energy Research Institute and Doosan Enerbility.

locator: Crossref bibliographic search this session found exact title match: Journal of Materials Processing Technology, vol 211, issue 5, pp 787-829, published May 2011, Elsevier BV; full 5-author list confirmed (Psyk, Risch, Kinsey, Tekkaya, Kleiner).
notes: Verifier found DOI and full author list via Crossref this session (scout had neither). Included despite 2011 date as the still-cited foundational review anchoring the 'EMF renaissance' claim.

title: Electromagnetic launchers for space applications
authors_or_org: Schroeder, J. M.; Gully, J. H.; Driga, M. D. (University of Texas at Austin)
claim_supported: Describes two NASA-Langley-sponsored electromagnetic launcher concepts (coilgun and railgun) designed to accelerate 10-14 kg payloads to 6-11 km/s for hypervelocity flight testing, with a proposed reconfigured version capable of placing 150 kg payloads into low Earth orbit -- the technical precedent cited by current Chinese (Galactic Energy) and other electromagnetic-launch-assist space-launch efforts.
locator: Crossref REST API record confirms DOI 10.1109/20.22590: IEEE Transactions on Magnetics, vol 25, issue 1, pp 504-507, 1989; NASA NTRS citation 19890044698 (re-fetched directly by verifier this session, confirming title/authors/abstract).

Both NASA NTRS and Crossref independently confirm identity.

title: China plans maglev rocket launch pad (Ziyang electromagnetic launch test bed; Galactic Energy)
authors_or_org: Ziyang Commercial Space Launch Technology Research Institute (四川资阳商业航天发射技术研究院) / China Aerospace Science and Industry Corporation (CASIC); reported by South China Morning Post
claim_supported: A Sichuan-based electromagnetic launch test bed (Ziyang Institute, with CASIC) completed its first full-system test on April 15, 2025, accelerating a 1.4-meter-diameter rocket model to supersonic speed using superconducting magnets; Ziyang Institute president Li Ping states the approach could 'double payload capacity and lower launch cost.' Galactic Energy (18 successful launches, 77 satellites deployed as of the article) is separately developing the Ceres-2 rocket to exploit this launch-assist approach, targeting the world's first operational electromagnetic-launch-assisted orbital rocket by 2028.
locator: South China Morning Post article 3303761, fetched directly by verifier this session; corroborates and supersedes the original orbitaltoday.com source (which returned HTTP 403 to both scout and verifier fetch attempts this session).
notes: SOURCE SWAP: original url (orbitaltoday.com) could not be fetched by either the scout or verifier (403 both times); verifier located and fetched an SCMP article covering the same event with matching facts (test date, institute, 2028 target), and substituted it as the primary citation since it is both higher-quality (major international newspaper, T2 vs the original T3) and independently fetched. Tier upgraded T3->T2 accordingly. No official Chinese-government or CASIC primary source was found/fetched this session.

authors_or_org: Gadiyar, Nishanth; Van Verdeghem, Joachim; Severson, Eric L.
claim_supported: Comprehensive review of axial-flux permanent-magnet (AFPM) machine design variants, sizing approaches, computationally efficient design-optimization techniques, and manufacturing methods -- the core technology behind the eVTOL/aerospace axial-flux-motor demand signal (e.g., Evolito, L16-047 reference in scout brief).
locator: Crossref REST API record confirms: IEEE Transactions on Industry Applications, vol 59, issue 4, pp 3920-3933, published July 2023; authors Nishanth Gadiyar and Eric L. Severson (University of Wisconsin-Madison), Joachim Van Verdeghem (UC Louvain).
notes: CORRECTION: scout's authors_or_org listed 'Nishanth, F.' -- verifier's Crossref confirmation shows the correct name is 'Nishanth Gadiyar' (family name Gadiyar, not 'Nishanth'); corrected.

authors_or_org: Unconfirmed authors (IEEE Transactions on Transportation Electrification)

The 'subsequent' framing was factually backwards and has been corrected/removed.

title: Application of Halbach magnet array to increase torque in axial-gap motors (ハルバッハ型磁石配置の適用によるアキシャルギャップモータの高トルク化)
authors_or_org: Nakanishi, Yuki; Morimoto, Shigeo; Sanada, Masayuki; Inoue, Yukinori (Osaka Metropolitan University); Tamamura, Shuhei; Maetani, Tatsuo (WOLONG Motor Control Technology Co., Ltd.)
claim_supported: Applies a Halbach magnet-array configuration to a 16-pole/12-slot axial-gap motor model, reportedly increasing torque by 21.1% versus the conventional-array baseline model -- a Japanese-language technical contribution to axial-gap motor torque-density improvement, presented at an IEEJ joint technical-committee meeting.
locator: IEEJ Electronic Library (電気学会電子図書館) document IEEJ-20230809X10301-004; fetched directly by verifier this session via the ieej.bookpark.ne.jp redirect.
rejection_reason: Verifier's direct fetch this session confirmed this is a 研究会資料 (technical-committee working paper) from the '2023年8月9日回転機/リニアドライブ/家電・民生合同研究会' (Aug 9, 2023 Rotating Machinery/Linear Drive/Home Appliance Joint Research Committee meeting), paper numbers RM23050/LD23064/HCA23034, pp 19-24 -- NOT a peer-reviewed 電気学会論文誌 (IEEJ Transactions) journal article. IEEJ treats 研究会資料 as a materially less-reviewed category (akin to a conference working paper), which fails the mission's peer-review academic-acceptance rule.
notes: Scout correctly flagged this venue as ambiguous and withheld a T1 upgrade; verifier's direct fetch resolved the ambiguity in favor of 'technical-committee working paper,' confirming the scout's caution was warranted. Full author list and affiliations now confirmed (Osaka Metropolitan University; WOLONG Motor Control Technology Co., Ltd.).
